data.title: Primior's GAIA raises $1.9M in 30 days
data.description:
• Primior (GRLT) said that its new venture, GAIA Marketplace, an RWA investment platform powered by AI and blockchain, raised $1.9 million in 30 days for its first offering.  
  • The Lumen multifamily secured commitments reaching 1900% of its initial funding target of $100,000.
  • Coinciding with the platform’s full activation, GAIA received strong interest from the capital market since its launch.

data.title: Trump calls for backing of Credit Card Competition Act
data.description:
• President Donald Trump urged support for the Credit Card Competition Act, saying the legislation would help curb what he described as “out of control” swipe fees, in a post on Truth Social.
  • “Everyone should support great Republican Senator Roger Marshall’s Credit Card Competition Act, in order to stop the out of control Swipe Fee ripoff,” Trump wrote on Truth Social.
  • Credit card and credit card issuer stocks fell on Monday after President Donald Trump late on Friday called for a one-year cap on credit card interest rates at 10%.

data.title: Oak Valley gets new marketing chief
data.description:
• Oak Valley Bancorp (OVLY), the bank holding company for Oak Valley Community Bank and its Eastern Sierra Community Bank division said that Bill Nunes has been promoted to executive VP, CMO, and Jose Sabala to senior VP and community reinvestment act officer.
  • Nunes joined the bank in 2005 and most recently served as senior VP, Marketing Manager, overseeing both the marketing and Community Reinvestment Act departments.

data.title: Delaware top court reduces Johnson & Johnson's $1B loss from robotics takeover - report
SK Hynix to invest $13B in South Korean AI chip packaging plant
data.description:
SK Hynix (HXSC.F) plans to invest 19 trillion Korean won ($12.9 billion) to build a new advanced packaging plant in the Korean city of Cheongju, with construction set to begin in April.

The new facility will build on the company’s existing footprint in the city and help boost production to meet rising demands tied to artificial intelligence.

It will focus on advanced packaging, which involves combining multiple memory chips into a single, high-density unit to improve performance and energy efficiency while reducing overall size.

SK Hynix has been working to boost its supply of high-bandwidth memory ((HBM)) for Nvidia and others to meet rising demand. The South Korea-based memory maker said in September that it had completed development and finished preparation for mass production of HBM4, the next-generation memory product vital for AI work.

The company competes with domestic competitor Samsung (SSNLF) and U.S.-based Micron (MU) in the memory space. Samsung (SSNLF) has also announced plans to ramp up HBM production in recent months.

data.title: Jeanine Pirro stands by Powell probe as criticism grows
data.description:
• U.S. attorney Jeanine Pirro didn’t seek sign-off from her bosses at the Justice Department before subpoenaing the Federal Reserve and has no plans to back down from an investigation into Chairman Jerome Powell, Bloomberg News reported on Tuesday, citing a person familiar with the matter.
  • Pirro posted on X Monday evening that her office had contacted the Federal Reserve "on multiple occasions to discuss cost overruns and the chairman’s congressional testimony, but were ignored, necessitating the use of legal process — which is not a threat."
  • The probe elicited objections from lawmakers and comments from President Donald Trump that suggest he’s trying to distance himself from the issue.
  • Nonetheless, Pirro, a former Fox News host who was confirmed as the US attorney for the District of Columbia five months ago, plans to forge ahead.

data.title: UBS CEO Sergio Ermotti plans to step down in April 2027, FT reports
data.description:
• UBS (UBS) chief executive Sergio Ermotti is planning to step down in April 2027, the Financial Times reported on Tuesday, citing people familiar with the matter.
  • Ermotti’s expected departure around next year’s annual meeting is set to formally start the search for a successor to one of banking’s most sought-after jobs, the report said.
  • Aleksandar Ivanovic, UBS’s asset management chief, has emerged as one of the executives most likely to succeed Ermotti.
  • The other candidates include UBS’s wealth management co-heads Iqbal Khan and Robert Karofsky, along with COO Bea Martin, FT added.

data.title: CME to adjust precious metals margin requirements amid price volatility
data.description:
• Exchange operator CME Group (CME) to change the way it sets margins for gold (XAUUSD:CUR), silver (XAGUSD:CUR), platinum (XPTUSD:CUR), and palladium (XPDUSD:CUR) futures following a surge in prices and volatile trading, it  said on Tuesday.
  • Under the new approach, margins will be calculated as a percentage of notional value rather than a fixed dollar amount, CME said in a notice.
  • The rates will be effective after the close of business on January 13, 2026.
 

ETFs related to gold, gold miners, silver, and silver miners: (GLD), (IAU), (SGOL), (OUNZ), (BAR), (GDX), (GDXJ), (NUGT), (RING), (DUST), (SLV), (SIVR), (AGQ), (ZSL), (PSLV), (SIL), (SILJ), and (SLVP).

data.title: Innventure prices $40M stock offering
data.description:
• Innventure (INV) has entered into securities purchase agreements with four institutional investors for the purchase and sale of 11.43M shares of common stock for gross proceeds of around $40M.
  • The company expects to use the net proceeds from this offering to repay all outstanding obligations under the convertible debentures, subject to the terms of the securities purchase agreement, dated September 15, 2025, and for working capital and general corporate purposes, which may include the repayment of certain other outstanding indebtedness and the ability of the company to exercise its right to receive equity in Accelsius in lieu of cash for Accelsius’ repayment of ~$8M principal amount of intercompany convertible debt and interest thereon.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about January 14, 2026, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Exxon remains “interested” in sending assessment team to Venezuela- report
data.description:
Exxon Mobil (XOM) has signaled continued interest in sending an assessment team to Venezuela for evaluating oil infrastructure and other assets, according to Reuters, citing an unnamed source.

The report comes days after U.S. President Donald Trump reached out to executives from more than 20 U.S. energy companies in a live-streamed meeting Friday, with Exxon CEO Darren Woods saying that Venezuela is “uninvestable” without significant changes to the country's commercial frameworks, legal system, and hydrocarbon laws.

Trump later said that he was "inclined" toward blocking the company from drilling in Venezuela, despite Woods noting that he was "confident … that those changes can be put in place" with the current administration working with the Venezuelan government.

Exxon (XOM) executives were surprised by these events, the source told Reuters.

Woods said Friday that Exxon (XOM) could have a technical team visit to assess the current state of Venezuelan assets in the coming weeks, and could assist in getting Venezuelan crude to market through its integrated businesses, which include refining and trading.

The meeting, which took place after the U.S. removed Venezuelan President Nicolas Madura, also had executives from ConocoPhillips (COP), Chevron (CVX), and other U.S. energy companies. Exxon, Chevron and Conoco had operations in the country, but had their assets seized as Venezuela nationalized the industry. Only Chevron remained in a deal with the country.
